chief_complaint: A 55-year-old male presented with severe chest pain following a bee sting.
history: The patient had a history of allergies and experienced anaphylaxis after the bee sting. He had no prior history of cardiovascular disease. He was not taking any medications.
exam: Vital signs: BP 90/60 mmHg, HR 120 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed a patient in distress with a normal cardiac examination.
labs: ECG: ST-elevation MI in inferior and anterior leads. Bloods: Elevated troponin levels. Imaging: Coronary angiography revealed pathological findings.
